title: Overriding ✍️
description: Change inherited behaviors to perfectly fit the child class.

slides.title: I Want to do it MY Way 😤
slides.content:
# Changing the Rules

Sometimes, a parent class gives a child a method, but the child wants to do it slightly differently.

Imagine `class Bird extends Animal`. The `Animal` class has an `eat()` method that prints 'Nom nom'.
But for a Bird, you want it to print 'Peck peck'.

You can **Override** the parent's method!

slides.title: The `@override` tag 🏷️

quizData.question: What is the purpose of `@override`?

options.text: It intentionally replaces an inherited method with a custom version for the child class.
It prevents a method from running.

quizData.explanation: Overriding allows a subclass to provide a specific, modified implementation of a method that is already provided by its parent class.

slides.title: The `super` Keyword 🦸‍♀️

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to trigger the parent class's version of the draw() method.
_____.draw();
quizData.explanation: While 'this' refers to the current object, 'super' acts as a direct line referring explicitly to the Parent blueprint.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Notice how the subclass entirely redefines what it means to calculate speed!
